articles.title: Allen Wealth Management LLC Makes New Investment in Kimberly-Clark Corporation $KMB
articles.summary: Allen Wealth Management LLC has made a new investment in Kimberly-Clark Corporation (NYSE: KMB), purchasing over 11,000 shares valued at approximately $1.135 million, now representing 0.7% of its portfolio. Kimberly-Clark recently increased its quarterly dividend to $1.28 per share, offering a 5.3% annual yield, and reported Q4 EPS of $1.86, beating estimates. Analysts currently rate the stock as a "Hold" with an average price target of $115.

articles.title: e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) Given Average Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" by Analysts
articles.summary: e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) has received a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from 18 analysts, with an average one-year price target of $111. The company reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates with $1.24 EPS and $489.5 million in revenue, and provided optimistic FY2026 guidance. However, CEO Tarang Amin recently sold a significant portion of his direct ownership in the company.

articles.title: SN top 10: BJ’s Wholesale Club makes move to keep up with competitors
articles.summary: BJ’s Wholesale Club has extended its store hours to 10 p.m. Monday through Saturday and 9 p.m. on Sundays, following similar moves by competitors Sam’s Club and Costco. This change aims to keep the retailer competitive within the warehouse club sector. The article also lists other top grocery stories from the week, including Target's specialized approach to grocery, Kroger's legal issues, and grocery price drops.

articles.title: Target Corp. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
articles.summary: Target Corp. (TGT) shares fell 3.29% on Monday, closing at $117.88, despite a positive trading day for the broader market. The S&P 500 Index rose 1.02% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.63%. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for Target's stock.

articles.title: 3 Retail Stocks to Watch for a Post-Tax-Day Bump
articles.summary: As Tax Day passes, some retailers may see a boost from consumers spending their refunds. This article highlights three retail stocks – Target (TGT), Deckers Outdoor (DECK), and Best Buy (BBY) – that could experience short-term gains. Target needs a sales win after facing various challenges, Deckers is capitalizing on market share gains with brands like UGG and HOKA, and Best Buy could benefit if refund dollars are used for big-ticket electronics.
